2605.16750.title: UniER: A Unified Benchmark for Item-level and Path-level Exercise Recommendation
2605.16750.paper_summary: Personalized exercise recommendation dynamically aligns pedagogical resources with individual knowledge mastery, which is crucial for satisfying students' dynamic learning needs in modern education. The field is currently driven by two dominant paradigms: Item-Level Exercise Recommendation (ILER) optimizes for immediate single-step state transitions, while Path-Level Exercise Recommendation (PLER) constructs coherent learning paths to maximize cumulative gains. Despite sharing the same ultimate objective, disparate evaluation setups have kept these two lines of research isolated, hindering unified benchmarking and fair comparison. To fill the gap, in this paper, we present a Unified Benchmark for Exercise Recommendation (UniER), a comprehensive evaluation framework that unifies ILER and PLER. Specifically, we introduce Weighted Cognitive Gain (WCG) as a unified metric to measure cross-paradigm algorithmic performance. Our benchmark encompasses 9 datasets spanning four generation methods, facilitating the comparison of 18 representative ILER/PLER methods. Through multi-dimensional analyses covering effectiveness, generalizability, robustness, and efficiency, our results reveal the systematic dominance of PLER and expose the pedagogical failure of ILER's fragmented recommendations under extreme sparsity and noise. Furthermore, we provide an open-source codebase of UniER to foster reproducible research and outline potential directions for future investigations.
2605.16750.paper_summary_zh: 個性化的運動推薦動態地將教學資源與個體的知識掌握對齊，這對於滿足現代教育中學生的動態學習需求至關重要。該領域目前由兩個主要範式驅動：項目級運動推薦（ILER）針對即時單步狀態轉換進行優化，而路徑級運動推薦（PLER）則構建連貫的學習路徑以最大化累積收益。儘管兩者共享相同的最終目標，但不同的評估設置使這兩條研究線路相互隔離，妨礙了統一基準測試和公平比較。為填補這一空白，在本文中，我們提出了一個運動推薦的統一基準（UniER），這是一個綜合評估框架，統一了ILER和PLER。具體而言，我們引入加權認知增益（WCG）作為一個統一的指標來衡量跨範式算法性能。我們的基準涵蓋了9個數據集，跨越四種生成方法，促進了18種代表性的ILER/PLER方法的比較。通過涵蓋有效性、普遍性、穩健性和效率的多維分析，我們的結果揭示了PLER的系統性優勢，並暴露了ILER在極端稀疏和噪聲下的片段化推薦的教學失敗。此外，我們提供了UniER的開源代碼庫，以促進可重複的研究，並概述了未來研究的潛在方向。
2605.16750.author: Xinghe Cheng et.al.
2605.16750.authors: Xinghe Cheng, Guiyong Zhuang, Yusheng Xie, Jiapu Wang, Yixin Liu, Quanlong Guan, Liangda Fang, Shirui Pan
